verses.text: 'Cast thy bread upon the waters: for thou shalt find it after many days'; give to seven and to eight, for you know not what evil may come. Generosity is wise. Scatter good freely, trusting God to return it in His time.

'Cast thy bread upon the waters: for thou shalt find it after many days'; give to seven and to eight, for you know not what evil may come. Generosity is wise. Scatter good freely, trusting God to return it in His time.

'Cast thy bread upon the waters: for thou shalt find it after many days'; give to seven and to eight, for you know not what evil may come. Generosity is wise. Scatter good freely, trusting God to return it in His time.

He who watches the wind never sows; since you know not the works of God who makes all, 'in the morning sow thy seed,' and in the evening too. Do not wait for perfect conditions. Diligent, persistent work leaves the increase to God.

He who watches the wind never sows; since you know not the works of God who makes all, 'in the morning sow thy seed,' and in the evening too. Do not wait for perfect conditions. Diligent, persistent work leaves the increase to God.

He who watches the wind never sows; since you know not the works of God who makes all, 'in the morning sow thy seed,' and in the evening too. Do not wait for perfect conditions. Diligent, persistent work leaves the increase to God.
Light is sweet and life pleasant, yet a man should remember 'the days of darkness... shall be many.' Enjoy life, but soberly. The shadow of death tempers our gladness with wisdom.
Light is sweet and life pleasant, yet a man should remember 'the days of darkness... shall be many.' Enjoy life, but soberly. The shadow of death tempers our gladness with wisdom.

Rejoice, young man, in your youth, but 'know thou, that for all these things God will bring thee into judgment'; so put away sorrow and evil. Youth is a gift to enjoy responsibly. Every pleasure is to be weighed against the coming reckoning.
